reasoning: Iteration 1: Initial query generation phase. The learner has provided comprehensive implementation strategies leveraging sorted columns (l_shipdate, o_orderdate) for aggressive row group pruning, column projection (38-75% reduction), and optimal join ordering. Q6 has highest optimization potential (83% row group pruning + 75% column projection). Implementing in recommended order: Q6 (simplest, validates ScanWithDateFilter), Q1 (validates HashAgg), Q3 (most complex, validates joins).

query_guidance.Q1: Implement using ScanWithDateFilter for l_shipdate <= '1998-09-02' (date32 <= 10471) with row group pruning (eliminates ~17 late row groups). Project 7 of 16 columns (l_shipdate, l_returnflag, l_linestatus, l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax) for 38% I/O reduction. Use arrow::compute::greater_equal for date filtering. Aggregate with HashAgg on (l_returnflag, l_linestatus) expecting ~4 groups. Compute 8 aggregates: SUM(qty), SUM(extendedprice), SUM(disc_price), SUM(charge), AVG(qty), AVG(price), AVG(discount), COUNT(*). Handle decimal128 to float64 conversion for arithmetic.
query_guidance.Q3: Join order: (1) Build hash on filtered customer (c_mktsegment='BUILDING', ~30K rows), (2) Probe with filtered orders (o_orderdate < date32 9204), (3) Build hash on join result (~100K), (4) Probe with filtered lineitem (l_shipdate > date32 9204). Parquet optimization: orders row group pruning keeps RG 0-66 of 200 (67% skip), lineitem row group pruning skips RG 0-400 of 1200 (33% skip), customer full scan with in-memory c_mktsegment filter. Column projection: customer 2/8 cols (c_custkey, c_mktsegment), orders 4/9 cols (o_orderkey, o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority), lineitem 4/16 cols (l_orderkey, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_shipdate). Use arrow::compute for all filters and revenue calculation (multiply + subtract). Aggregate on (l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority) expecting ~10K groups, then sort by revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC, limit 10.
query_guidance.Q6: HIGHEST IMPACT optimization. Use ScanWithDateFilter for l_shipdate range [date32 8766, 9131) covering 1994-01-01 to 1994-12-31. Row group pruning: skip ~83% of 1200 row groups (read only ~200 RG overlapping 1994). Column projection: read only 4/16 columns (l_shipdate, l_quantity, l_discount, l_extendedprice) for 75% I/O reduction. After row group pruning, apply in-memory filters using arrow::compute: l_discount BETWEEN 0.05 AND 0.07 (use greater_equal + less_equal + and_kleene), l_quantity < 24 (use less), combine all with and_kleene. Use arrow::compute::filter, then arrow::compute::multiply for l_extendedprice * l_discount. Compute single scalar SUM aggregate. Expected: 1M rows after row group pruning → ~100K after in-memory filters.
